CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-16-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-05-2017 Continued To Jul-05-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order On Ruling On Demurrer
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Linda Von Hurst's motion for reconsideration of the March 8, 2017 order sustaining the demurrer to the first amended complaint without leave to amend is denied. Without regard to compliance with the requirements of CCP 1008, the court has reconsidered the March 8 order and remains of the view that that order was correctly decided. Order and Judgment signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Order On Ruling On Demurrer
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Continued To May-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Richard S. Trugman, of Trugman Law Group, APC, Tel. 310.273.8834, for plaintiffs;
Keith M. Velleca, of Law Offices of Keith M. Velleca, Tel. 415.293.8048, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT IRENE CHEUNG, RICHARD HUGHES VON HURST's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as to all three causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Linda Von Hurst is sustained without leave to amend as to all three causes of action. None of Ms. Von Hurst's claims states a cause of action nor is it reasonably possible that she can allege a viable claim. As to the first cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, Ms. Von Hurst fails to allege interference with a third party relationship. Ms. Von Hurst alleges an economic benefit from her former husband Mr. Von Hurst and that Mr. Von Hurst and Ms. Cheung conspired together to prevent Ms. Von Hurst's receipt of spousal support. Ms. Von Hurst also fails to allege wrongful conduct. The parties appear to agree that Mr. Von Hurst reached retirement age before the judgment was entered in the divorce proceedings. Once reaching retirement age, Mr. Von Hurst was no longer obligated to work. (In re Marriage of Reynolds (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1373, 1378.) Ms. Von Hurst also fails to cite any authority holding that it is wrongful for Ms. Cheung to provide financial support to Mr. Von Hurst. As to the second cause of action for "interference with prospective civil litigation" against Ms. Cheung, there is no such claim. Ms. Von Hurst cites sections of a Witkin treatise that relate to the tort of intentional interference with prospective advantage or business relations. The second cause of action appears to be a repackaging of the first cause of action with the additional nonactionable allegation that Ms. Cheung failed to comply with discovery requests in the divorce action. Ms. Cheung characterizes her second cause of action as a spoliation claim per Smith v. Superior Court (1984) 151 Cal.App.3d 491, 496. Cedars-Sinai Med. Center v. Superior Court (1998) 18 Cal.4th 1, 7 disapproved of first party intentional spoliation claims. Temple Community Hosp. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 464 held that the tort of intentional spoliation also does not apply to a third party. The third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails to allege outrageous conduct as a matter of law. Order signed. The Court will allow 30 days before signing the judgment so that counsel may file a motion under CCP 1008. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2017 Continued To Mar-08-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
